meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve no marketing skills (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome no marketing skills. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve No Marketing Skills
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about marketing. Learn how to build a high-impact portfolio using Antigravity and Claude Code to showcase your AI engineering skills to recruiters.
content.answer_block: You don't need marketing skills; you need a 'Proof of Work' portfolio. By leveraging AI-native tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can ship production-ready applications that demonstrate technical competence. Your code is your marketing. Build, document, and deploy projects that solve real problems to attract employers and collaborators naturally.
content.intro:
The silence of a launched project that no one visits is a rite of passage for every developer. You’ve spent weeks coding, yet the lack of marketing skills leaves your work buried in the digital void. It feels like you’re shouting into a hurricane, waiting for a signal that never comes.

But here is the secret: you aren't a marketer; you’re an AI builder. Your portfolio is your leverage. By shifting your focus from 'getting traffic' to 'demonstrating mastery,' you transform your projects into high-fidelity resumes that speak for themselves. Let’s use Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your raw technical potential into a career-defining asset.

steps.title: Define a High-Utility Project
steps.description: Select a niche problem that an existing SaaS fails to solve. This proves you can identify technical gaps.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Engineer. Suggest 3 high-impact, small-scope AI project ideas that solve specific, measurable problems for developers. Focus on utility over novelty.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a README.md structure for a high-impact portfolio project that highlights technical architecture.'

steps.title: Architect with AI-Assisted Speed
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scaffold your backend, ensuring best practices like error handling and modularity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, production-ready directory structure for an AI-powered SaaS using Next.js and FastAPI. Include a clear separation of concerns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Create the project directory structure and initialize the base configuration files.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic
steps.description: Focus on the 'Engine' of your app. This is the code that recruiters evaluate to judge your engineering maturity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core logic for an AI agent that processes user input and returns structured data. Use Pydantic for validation and include robust error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Implement the core agent logic and add unit tests for the main function.'

steps.title: Build 'Proof of Work' Documentation
steps.description: Document your design decisions. Explaining *why* you chose a technology is more valuable than the code itself.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a technical 'Decision Log' for a project. Explain why I chose specific libraries and how I handled API rate limiting. Make it sound professional and reflective.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Update the documentation to include a technical decision log based on current project files.'

steps.title: Automated Deployment and Showcase
steps.description: Deploy your app to make it accessible. A live URL is worth 1,000 lines of GitHub code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a step-by-step guide to deploying this project on Vercel or Railway with environment variable security best practices.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Configure the deployment pipeline and generate a summary of the live project features for a portfolio landing page.'

pro_tips: Focus on 'depth of integration'—a simple app that connects three APIs perfectly is better than a complex app with a buggy UI.
Always include a 'Lessons Learned' section in your README; it shows you have a growth mindset, which is what senior leads look for.
Use Claude Code to refactor your code for readability; clean, readable code is a sign of a professional engineer.
Host your project live. Recruiters will rarely download and run your repo locally, but they will click a live demo link.
Record a 60-second video demo. It bypasses the need for the recruiter to understand your code at all—they can see the value immediately.
content.next_steps: After completing your first project, iterate on the feedback loop by adding a new feature every week. Once you have three polished projects, start contributing to open-source repositories to build your professional network.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the strategy, and the roadmap. The gap between where you are and where you want to be is just a few hundred lines of code away. Stop waiting for permission to build. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
